expectations.text: Explains all three impact levels: Low, Moderate, High
expectations.evidence: Response has dedicated sections for each of the three legacy impact levels with definitions, characteristics, and examples, plus a CR26 class mapping table.

expectations.text: References FIPS 199 as the basis for impact categorization
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly names 'FIPS 199' as the foundational standard and explains its three dimensions (confidentiality, integrity, availability) and the high-water mark rule.

expectations.text: States approximate number of controls for each level
expectations.evidence: Response states Low ≈ 156 controls, Moderate = 323 controls, High = 421 controls (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5), and LI-SaaS ≈ 47 controls, all in a comparison table.

expectations.text: Addresses law enforcement data sensitivity and likely High impact designation
expectations.evidence: Response has a dedicated section 'Your Situation: Law Enforcement Sensitive Data' asserting High/Class C designation with detailed reasoning about confidentiality requirements and CR26 Class C alignment.

expectations.text: Mentions LI-SaaS as a special category for Low impact
expectations.evidence: Response includes a sub-section 'FedRAMP Low Impact' covering LI-SaaS as a special category, describing its ~47 controls, eligibility criteria (no PII, no federal data storage), and limitations.
